ciphertext_sha256_purpose: transport and remote recovery integrity only
protocol: Derive processed_snapshot_root deterministically from the committed Processed snapshot.
List the fixed Release assets and fail closed if the count is greater than one.

When encrypted private publish state names the same snapshot and one asset exists, download it, verify ciphertext_sha256, decrypt it and verify timeline_plaintext_sha256; if all checks pass, perform no render and no mutation.
Otherwise render deterministic PNG bytes in the ephemeral runner and compute timeline_plaintext_sha256 before encryption.
If the existing asset decrypts to the same timeline_plaintext_sha256, repair encrypted publish state if needed and perform no asset mutation.
For changed plaintext, age-encrypt the candidate with expected fresh randomness, decrypt it in the same job and verify its plaintext digest before any remote mutation.
If one current asset exists, download and verify it in ephemeral storage, then delete that fixed remote asset.
Upload the candidate under the same fixed name and verify remote size, ciphertext_sha256 and decryptable plaintext_sha256.
After remote verification, write encrypted private publish state for the new snapshot and asset identity in one repository commit.
On failure after deletion, publish TIMELINE_REPAIR_REQUIRED with asset count zero; never persist a second backup image.
The next run must rebuild from the same committed Processed snapshot before any later snapshot can publish.
forbidden: temporary or historical Release image assets
using age ciphertext equality as the no-change oracle
